name: Ahsen
title: Director of Products
challenge:
My  portal has a solid technical foundation — TLS termination via Caddy, bearer token authentication, Docker container isolation, HMAC-validated webhooks, and file size controls. However, it currently has critical and high-severity gaps that would cause it to fail any ISO 27001 or SOC 2 audit.
The gaps fall into four categories:
•	Misconfigured security controls — CORS wildcard, token exposed in URLs and logs, no security headers
•	Missing technical controls — no rate limiting, no MIME validation, no structured audit logging
•	No governance documentation — no ISMS, no risk register, no incident response plan
•	No privacy framework — no data map, no retention policy, no deletion mechanism

The whole thing should be according to following standards
ISO/IEC 27001
ISO/IEC 27002
ISO/IEC 27034
ISO/IEC 27017
ISO/IEC 27701
ISO/IEC 25010

I need a whole plan to implement it

name: Mark Rewers
challenge: Your governance model already controls how agents behave, how actions are verified, and how decisions are audited. Waht is the raodmap for the next natural step, which is defining a structured operational state layer so that agents reason over a consistent representation of the environment rather than reconstructing context from documents each session.

name: Waqas
title: Product Owner
challenge: The audit trail records everything, but who reviews those logs day to day? Is it automatic or does a human actually look at them?

name: Waqas
title: Product Owner
challenge: You mention violations are structurally impossible, but what happens the very first time a violation actually does occur? What is the process?

name: Waqas
title: Product Owner
challenge: How does the governance model handle a situation where a user intentionally tries to use their AI to do something unethical, is there protection against that?

title: CTO
